expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly reads 'deriva://catalog/features' and 'deriva://table/Image/feature-values/newest' before creating anything, with the note 'Assuming no existing Diagnosis or Model_Prediction features are found, proceed.'
Step 2 calls create_vocabulary('Diagnosis_Type') followed by add_term for all 5 categories (Normal, Pneumonia, COVID-19, Tuberculosis, Lung_Cancer) before any create_feature call in Step 3.
Step 3 calls create_feature(table_name='Image', feature_name='Diagnosis', terms=['Diagnosis_Type'], ...) referencing the vocabulary created in Step 2.

The response explicitly states 'Each radiologist runs their own execution so annotations are independently attributed' and 'Each execution is independently attributed in the Execution column of every feature value record.' It also mentions features are multivalued and support inter-annotator agreement analysis.
Step 6 calls create_execution and start_execution before add_feature_value_record, then stop_execution after. Same pattern in Step 8 for model predictions.

The response creates a separate Model_Prediction feature with a float4 'confidence' metadata column: metadata=[{"name": "confidence", "type": {"typename": "float4"}, ...}], and discusses this in the design analysis and Key Design Decisions section.

The design analysis explicitly separates 'Radiologist diagnostic labels' and 'Model confidence scores' as 'two distinct annotation acts from different sources', creating separate features (Diagnosis and Model_Prediction) and separate workflows (Radiologist Annotation vs. Chest X-Ray Classifier Inference) with separate executions.
